The in-order successor is the node with the smallest value strictly greater than p's value.
In a BST, if the current node's value is greater than p.val, it might be the successor — but search left for a closer one.
If the current node's value is less than or equal to p.val, the successor must be in the right subtree.
You can solve this in O(H) time by using the BST property to narrow the search, without performing a full traversal.
